blinky — Entity

Source file: src/blinky.vhd.

ENTITY blinky IS

SPHINX-VHDL: Entity was not found in parsed VHDL files!

Annotated Source

src/blinky.vhd
  1library ieee;
  2use ieee.std_logic_1164.all;
  3use ieee.numeric_std.all;
  4
  5-- Blinky LED controller.
  6--
  7-- Implements a 3-state FSM that drives an LED through a repeating
  8-- ON -> FADE -> OFF sequence. Each state is held for a configurable
  9-- number of clock cycles determined by an external timer pulse.
 10--
 11-- State sequence: ON -> FADE -> OFF -> ON -> ...
 12entity blinky is
 13    port (
 14        -- System clock, rising-edge triggered.
 15        clk       : in  std_logic;
 16        -- Synchronous active-high reset. Returns FSM to OFF state.
 17        rst       : in  std_logic;
 18        -- Enable signal. FSM only advances when en is asserted.
 19        en        : in  std_logic;
 20        -- Phase timer expiry pulse. Must be high for exactly one cycle.
 21        timer_exp : in  std_logic;
 22        -- LED full-brightness drive.
 23        led_on    : out std_logic;
 24        -- LED half-brightness PWM drive (fade effect).
 25        led_fade  : out std_logic;
 26        -- LED off indicator (all drives deasserted).
 27        led_off   : out std_logic
 28    );
 29end entity blinky;
 30
 31-- RTL implementation using a 3-process Moore FSM style.
 32architecture rtl of blinky is
 33
 34    -- FSM state type encoding the three LED phases.
 35    type t_state is (
 36        -- LED fully illuminated.
 37        ON_STATE,
 38        -- LED dimming via PWM (fade transition).
 39        FADE_STATE,
 40        -- LED completely off.
 41        OFF_STATE
 42    );
 43
 44    -- Current state register, initialised to OFF at power-on.
 45    signal state      : t_state := OFF_STATE;
 46    -- Next state combinational signal.
 47    signal next_state : t_state;
 48
 49begin
 50
 51    -- p_state_reg: Clocked state register.
 52    --
 53    -- Captures next_state on each rising edge of clk.
 54    -- Synchronous reset drives state back to OFF_STATE.
 55    --
 56    -- .. wavedrom::
 57    --
 58    --    { "signal": [
 59    --      { "name": "clk",       "wave": "P......." },
 60    --      { "name": "rst",       "wave": "1.0....." },
 61    --      { "name": "en",        "wave": "0......." },
 62    --      { "name": "state",     "wave": "=.=.....", "data": ["(any)","OFF"] },
 63    --      { "name": "led_on",    "wave": "x.0....." },
 64    --      { "name": "led_fade",  "wave": "x.0....." },
 65    --      { "name": "led_off",   "wave": "x.1....." }
 66    --    ]}
 67    p_state_reg : process(clk)
 68    begin
 69        if rising_edge(clk) then
 70            if rst = '1' then
 71                state <= OFF_STATE;
 72            else
 73                state <= next_state;
 74            end if;
 75        end if;
 76    end process p_state_reg;
 77
 78    -- p_next_state: Combinational next-state logic.
 79    --
 80    -- FSM only advances when both en and timer_exp are asserted.
 81    -- If en is deasserted the state holds regardless of timer_exp.
 82    --
 83    -- Transitions:
 84    --   OFF_STATE  + en + timer_exp -> ON_STATE
 85    --   ON_STATE   + en + timer_exp -> FADE_STATE
 86    --   FADE_STATE + en + timer_exp -> OFF_STATE
 87    --
 88    -- .. wavedrom::
 89    --
 90    --    { "signal": [
 91    --      { "name": "clk",       "wave": "P............." },
 92    --      { "name": "en",        "wave": "01...........0" },
 93    --      { "name": "timer_exp", "wave": "0..1..1..1..0." },
 94    --      { "name": "state",     "wave": "=..=..=..=..=.",
 95    --        "data": ["OFF","ON","FADE","OFF","OFF"] },
 96    --      { "name": "led_on",    "wave": "0..1..0..0..0." },
 97    --      { "name": "led_fade",  "wave": "0..0..1..0..0." },
 98    --      { "name": "led_off",   "wave": "1..0..0..1..1." }
 99    --    ]}
100    p_next_state : process(state, en, timer_exp)
101    begin
102        next_state <= state;
103
104        if en = '1' and timer_exp = '1' then
105            case state is
106                when OFF_STATE  => next_state <= ON_STATE;
107                when ON_STATE   => next_state <= FADE_STATE;
108                when FADE_STATE => next_state <= OFF_STATE;
109            end case;
110        end if;
111    end process p_next_state;
112
113    -- p_outputs: Combinational Moore output decode.
114    --
115    -- Drives LED outputs based solely on current state.
116    -- All outputs default to 0 before the case statement to
117    -- prevent latches during synthesis.
118    --
119    -- Output truth table:
120    --   OFF_STATE  -> led_on=0 led_fade=0 led_off=1
121    --   ON_STATE   -> led_on=1 led_fade=0 led_off=0
122    --   FADE_STATE -> led_on=0 led_fade=1 led_off=0
123    --
124    -- .. wavedrom::
125    --
126    --    { "signal": [
127    --      { "name": "state",    "wave": "=.=.=.=.",
128    --        "data": ["OFF","ON","FADE","OFF"] },
129    --      { "name": "led_on",   "wave": "0.1.0.0." },
130    --      { "name": "led_fade", "wave": "0.0.1.0." },
131    --      { "name": "led_off",  "wave": "1.0.0.1." }
132    --    ]}
133    p_outputs : process(state)
134    begin
135        led_on   <= '0';
136        led_fade <= '0';
137        led_off  <= '0';
138
139        case state is
140            when OFF_STATE  => led_off  <= '1';
141            when ON_STATE   => led_on   <= '1';
142            when FADE_STATE => led_fade <= '1';
143        end case;
144    end process p_outputs;
145
146end architecture rtl;
